findings.title: Required Sections Present
findings.finding: The draft does not include a clearly labeled Related Documents section.
findings.evidence: No heading or clearly identifiable section for related documents was found in the uploaded Google Doc.
findings.suggested_enhancement: Add a short Related Documents section near the end of the document.
findings.suggested_language: Related Documents: Vendor Due Diligence Standard; Third-Party Risk Procedure.

findings.title: Retention Language Is Specific
findings.finding: The retention statement is too generic to be operationally useful.
findings.evidence: "Records will be retained as required by applicable law and company policy."
findings.suggested_enhancement: Replace the generic statement with language that points to the governing retention schedule or controlling standard.
findings.suggested_language: Records and evidence created under this Policy must be retained in accordance with the approved corporate retention schedule and any controlling records management standard.

findings.title: Purpose and Scope Are Distinct and Clear
findings.finding: Purpose and scope are distinguishable and understandable.
findings.evidence: The draft includes a short Purpose section and a separate Scope section describing the businesses and activities covered.

findings.title: Owner and Review Cadence Are Explicit
findings.finding: The owner and annual review expectation are both explicit.
findings.evidence: "Document Owner: Global Compliance Policy Lead" and "This Policy will be reviewed at least annually."

findings.title: Related Documents Are Complete and Consistent
findings.finding: The draft references a Standard in the body but does not list it in a dedicated related-documents section.
findings.evidence: "See the Vendor Due Diligence Standard for minimum diligence requirements."
findings.suggested_enhancement: List the referenced Standard and Procedure together in a consistent format.
findings.suggested_language: Related Documents: Vendor Due Diligence Standard; Third-Party Risk Procedure.

findings.title: Policy Language Avoids Excessive Procedure Detail
findings.finding: One section includes step-by-step instructions that read more like a procedure than a policy requirement.
findings.evidence: "First, the reviewer opens the vendor file. Next, the reviewer updates the checklist. Then, the reviewer emails Legal for sign-off."
findings.suggested_enhancement: Convert the section into a policy-level expectation and move detailed steps to a procedure or standard.
findings.suggested_language: Business teams must complete and document required third-party diligence steps before onboarding, using the approved standard and procedure.

findings.title: Requirement Language Is Clear
findings.finding: Some obligation statements use weak language such as 'should consider' where mandatory language may be more appropriate.
findings.evidence: "Business teams should consider enhanced diligence for high-risk vendors."
findings.suggested_enhancement: Use clear requirement language where the obligation is mandatory.
findings.suggested_language: Business teams must perform enhanced diligence for high-risk vendors in accordance with the approved standard.

findings.title: External Authority References Are Anchored
findings.finding: The draft references applicable law and regulatory expectations, but the citations remain high level.
findings.evidence: "This Policy supports compliance with applicable anti-corruption and third-party oversight requirements."
